2604.12482.title: Social Learning Strategies for Evolved Virtual Soft Robots
2604.12482.paper_summary: Optimizing the body and brain of a robot is a coupled challenge: the morphology determines what control strategies are effective, while the control parameters influence how well the morphology performs. This joint optimization can be done through nested loops of evolutionary and learning processes, where the control parameters of each robot are learned independently. However, the control parameters learned by one robot may contain valuable information for others. Thus, we introduce a social learning approach in which robots can exploit optimized parameters from their peers to accelerate their own brain optimization. Within this framework, we systematically investigate how the selection of teachers, deciding which and how many robots to learn from, affects performance, experimenting with virtual soft robots in four tasks and environments. In particular, we study the effect of inheriting experience from morphologically similar robots due to the tightly coupled body and brain in robot optimization. Our results confirm the effectiveness of building on others' experience, as social learning clearly outperforms learning from scratch under equivalent computational budgets. In addition, while the optimal teacher selection strategy remains open, our findings suggest that incorporating knowledge from multiple teachers can yield more consistent and robust improvements.
2604.12482.paper_summary_zh: 優化機器人的身體和大腦是一個相互關聯的挑戰：形態決定了哪些控制策略是有效的，而控制參數則影響形態的表現。這種聯合優化可以通過進化和學習過程的嵌套循環來完成，其中每個機器人的控制參數是獨立學習的。然而，一個機器人學到的控制參數可能對其他機器人包含有價值的信息。因此，我們引入了一種社會學習方法，讓機器人可以利用同伴的優化參數來加速自己的大腦優化。在這個框架內，我們系統地研究了教師的選擇，即決定從哪些和多少機器人學習，如何影響性能，並在四個任務和環境中對虛擬柔性機器人進行實驗。特別地，我們研究了由於機器人優化中身體和大腦的緊密耦合，從形態上相似的機器人繼承經驗的效果。我們的結果確認了基於他人經驗的有效性，因為社會學習在相同計算預算下明顯優於從零開始學習。此外，儘管最佳教師選擇策略仍然開放，我們的研究結果表明，從多位教師中獲取知識可以帶來更一致和穩健的改進。
2604.12482.author: K. Ege de Bruin et.al.
2604.12482.authors: K. Ege de Bruin, Kyrre Glette, Kai Olav Ellefsen, Giorgia Nadizar, Eric Medvet
